title: Rukmini Silverworks 925 jewelry catalog: batch isolate-on-pure-white with metal grade + consistent 3000px square e-com crops (transparent PNG + white-BG JPEG per SKU)
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief ("Nagpur Silver Jewelry Photoshoot"): the client has "925 sterling-silver jewellery that now need clean, consistent imagery for our online catalogue and marketplaces"; "so the metalwork stays the hero"; "each file has to be delivered on pure #FFFFFF with no visible shadows or colour cast"; "I'm comfortable if you run the clean-up through an AI workflow, Photoshop, or Lightroom—as long as the final images are crisp, evenly exposed and ready for immediate upload"; "High-resolution JPEGs (3,000 px on the long edge)". REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable, gen-free Adobe-only batch benchmark: the original brief bundles a physical Nagpur photoshoot (out of connector scope) with editing (in scope). This spec models the client as HANDING OFF six already-shot raw SKU frames (a normal editor hand-off / marketplace re-processing scenario) and scopes the job to the editing the brief actually rewards — isolate each piece on pure #FFFFFF, keep the silver as hero, remove any colour cast/shadow, and export consistent 3000x3000 web-ready crops. The real deliverable pattern (matching transparent PNGs + white-BG JPEGs, ~3000px, consistent square crop) is honored literally. The white backdrop is built the non-generative, palette-correct way (select the piece as subject, invert to a background mask, solid-fill that mask with #FFFFFF), and image_remove_background lifts the same graded master to a transparent PNG. NO generative fill, NO outpaint/expand, NO upscale, NO inpainting of occluded pixels. The real brand is replaced with the fictional "Rukmini Silverworks".
one_line_ask: Batch-process six raw 925-silver SKU photos into consistent, cast-free, pure-#FFFFFF e-commerce images — isolate each piece, grade the metal so it reads crisp, and deliver a transparent PNG plus a 3000x3000 white-background JPEG per SKU.
full_brief: Rukmini Silverworks, a boutique 925 sterling-silver house, is re-listing its collection across its own store and two marketplaces and needs clean, consistent product imagery so the metalwork stays the hero. The owner has handed off six raw tabletop frames shot on a phone under mixed studio light — a filigree pendant, a pair of jhumka drop earrings, a stacking band ring, a chain bracelet, an oxidised cuff, and a solitaire-look ring. As-shot they are inconsistent: slightly tilted, framed loose with a grey sweep and prop clutter visible, dull/flat exposure, and a warm-to-cool colour cast that makes the silver look muddy or yellow rather than bright neutral. The job is a batch clean-up, not a re-shoot: for every SKU (1) straighten and level the piece; (2) neutralise the white balance and lift the exposure so the silver reads as bright, cool-neutral metal with no colour cast; (3) gently push clarity/contrast and, on the oxidised piece, hold the dark detail so the antique recesses stay legible; (4) select the jewellery as the subject, invert that selection to a background-only mask, and solid-fill it with pure #FFFFFF so each piece sits on a flat, shadow-free white backdrop (the metalwork must keep its true edge — no halo, no chopped prongs, open chain-links and prong gaps preserved); (5) crop every SKU to an identical centered square and export at 3000 x 3000 px, sRGB, web-ready; (6) from the same graded master, strip the background to a clean transparent PNG for the store's own layered layouts. Deliverables per SKU: a transparent-background PNG and a 3000 x 3000 white-#FFFFFF JPEG, all six looking like a matched set. Brand constraint: pure #FFFFFF background (hex exact), no visible shadow, no colour cast, metal is the hero — the pieces must look like one consistent catalog, not six different edits.

inputs.name: sku01_filigree_pendant_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone macro of a 925 sterling-silver filigree pendant lying on a light-grey paper sweep, mixed tabletop studio light, slightly tilted (about 3 degrees), framed loose with a corner of a jewelry prop stand and a soft shadow visible, dull flat exposure, slightly warm/yellow colour cast on the metal, real brushed-metal texture, tiny dust specks, no studio gloss, candid product-shot look.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (tilt, loose frame, grey sweep, warm cast, flat tone) so the straighten/neutralise/select-invert-fill/isolate chain has real work to do; gpt-image-2 for photoreal metal. This is the hero SKU whose grade values set the reference for the set.

inputs.name: sku02_jhumka_earrings_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone macro of a pair of 925 silver jhumka drop earrings on a grey fabric surface, cool bluish colour cast from a window, underexposed shadows, slight tilt, a bit of lint and a prop clip visible at the edge, real granulation and bell-shaped metal detail, no CGI gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Cool cast + underexposure (opposite of sku01's warm cast) so white-balance neutralisation must be per-image; two earrings test subject-select on a multi-part piece.

inputs.name: sku03_stacking_band_ring_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone macro of a thin 925 silver stacking band ring standing on a small acrylic ring stand on a grey sweep, flat grey lighting, muddy midtones, tiny reflections, slightly off-level, background clutter of a second prop out of focus, real polished-metal texture, candid.
inputs.realism_notes: Small subject on a visible prop stand — subject-select must cleanly separate the ring from the acrylic stand so the inverted background mask (sweep + stand) fills to white; flat muddy tone justifies the neutralise/grade.

inputs.name: sku04_chain_bracelet_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone macro of a 925 silver curb-chain bracelet coiled on a light grey surface, mixed warm/cool light, dull flat contrast, faint drop shadow, a bit tilted, real interlocking-link metal texture and clasp detail, dust specks, no gloss retouch.
inputs.realism_notes: Open chain with gaps between links stresses subject-selection edge fidelity — the link gaps must fall in the inverted background mask (fill white / go transparent), not be filled as subject; faint shadow justifies the flat-white backdrop fill.

inputs.name: sku05_oxidised_cuff_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone macro of an oxidised 925 silver cuff bracelet with blackened antique detailing on a grey board, warm yellow cast, low contrast so the oxidised recesses look muddy, slight tilt, edge of a prop visible, real hammered/oxidised metal texture, candid product look.
inputs.realism_notes: Oxidised dark detail must stay legible after the grade (don't crush blacks) — justifies the extra dark-portions lift; warm cast to neutralise; tests that neutralise + dark-portions keeps antique detail before isolation.

inputs.name: sku06_solitaire_ring_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone macro of a 925 silver solitaire-look ring with a clear cubic-zirconia stone on a grey sweep, cool cast, flat exposure, slight tilt, small reflections and a prop shadow, real faceted-stone sparkle and polished-band metal, no CGI, candid.
inputs.realism_notes: Reflective stone + band tests subject-select and edge retention on a bright specular piece; sixth SKU completes the matched set.

inputs.name: rukmini_catalog_spec.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a short 6-line client spec note for a silver-jewelry catalog batch: background must be pure #FFFFFF (exact hex), no visible shadow, no colour cast, silver is the hero and must read bright/neutral, final crop is a centered square at 3000x3000 px sRGB, deliver both a transparent PNG and a white-BG JPEG per SKU, and the six images must look like one consistent set.
inputs.realism_notes: Plain client hand-off note pinning the exact brand constraints (hex, size, dual deliverable) the editor must hit; drives the crop target, the fill colour, and the QA pass; no merge fields.

outputs.name: sku_isolated_transparent_png_x6
outputs.spec: Six background-removed transparent PNGs (one per SKU), jewellery isolated with true metal edges (no halo, prongs/chain gaps preserved), graded to bright neutral silver; for the store's own layered layouts. Each stripped from the same per-SKU graded master via image_remove_background.

outputs.name: sku_whitebg_3000_jpg_x6
outputs.spec: Six 3000 x 3000 px sRGB JPEGs, each isolated piece on a pure #FFFFFF backdrop built by select-subject -> invert -> solid-fill of the background mask, flat and shadow-free, identical centered square crop; marketplace-ready and visually matched across the set.

connector_workflow.note: Create a Creative Cloud project folder to hold every SKU's raw, isolated, and white-BG output so the six-piece batch stays organised.
Level the ~3-degree tilt on the hero filigree-pendant frame so the piece sits square before selection and crop.
Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat/dull exposure.
Neutralise the warm/yellow cast so the silver reads as bright cool-neutral metal (the brief's 'no colour cast' requirement); this SKU sets the master white-balance reference for the set.
Nudge contrast/clarity so the metalwork pops and the fine filigree detail stays crisp (metal-is-hero); the values chosen here become the set's reference grade.
Auto-select the pendant as the subject, separating it from the grey sweep and prop clutter.
Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the filigree pendant) can be filled.

Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF (RGB 255/255/255) so the sweep becomes a uniform, shadow-free, cast-free white backdrop behind the pendant. SOLID fill only (no generative content).
Crop to a centered square and export at 3000x3000 sRGB - the fixed target applied identically to all six SKUs. White-BG JPEG #1.
Branch off the same graded master to strip the sweep to full transparency, keeping the true metal edge (no halo). Transparent PNG #1 for the store's layered layouts.
Auto-tone the jhumka earrings to lift the underexposed cool frame.
Neutralise the earrings' cool/bluish window cast (per-image WB, opposite direction to sku01) so the pair matches the set's neutral silver.
Select both earrings as the subject (multi-part piece) off the grey fabric.
Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the jhumka earrings) can be filled.
Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF behind the earrings (solid fill only).
Same 3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #2.
Strip the graded earrings to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #2.
Neutralise the flat/muddy stacking-ring frame to bright-neutral silver.
Select just the ring as the subject, separating it cleanly from the acrylic prop stand.
Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep and acrylic stand (everything except the stacking-band ring) can be filled.
Solid-fill the inverted background mask (grey sweep + acrylic stand) with pure #FFFFFF (solid fill only).
3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #3.
Strip the graded ring to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #3.
Neutralise the mixed warm/cool cast on the chain bracelet to match the set.
Select the curb chain as the subject; the open gaps between links must stay OUTSIDE the subject mask so they are treated as background.
Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask that includes the sweep AND the open link-gaps, so both fill to white / go transparent rather than being painted as metal.
Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF - including the link gaps, which read as background - so the bracelet sits on clean white with its open links preserved (solid fill only).
3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #4.
Strip the graded bracelet to a transparent PNG, preserving the see-through link gaps. Transparent PNG #4.
Neutralise the cuff's warm cast to match the set.
Lift the dark portions just enough that the blackened oxidised antique detail stays legible (no crushed blacks) while the bright silver stays neutral.
Select the cuff as the subject off the grey board.
Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the oxidised cuff) can be filled.
Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF behind the cuff (solid fill only).
3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #5.
Strip the graded cuff to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #5.
Neutralise the solitaire ring's cool cast while keeping the CZ stone's sparkle.
Select the reflective solitaire ring as the subject, retaining the specular band and faceted-stone edge.
Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the solitaire ring) can be filled.
Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF behind the ring (solid fill only).
3000x3000 centered-square crop/export completing the matched set. White-BG JPEG #6.
Strip the graded ring to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #6.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: sku01_transparent_png, sku02_transparent_png, sku03_transparent_png, sku04_transparent_png, sku05_transparent_png, sku06_transparent_png, sku01_whitebg_3000, sku02_whitebg_3000, sku03_whitebg_3000, sku04_whitebg_3000, sku05_whitebg_3000, sku06_whitebg_3000
connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for all twelve finished deliverables (six transparent PNGs + six white-BG JPEGs) into the project folder.
Finalize the upload against the project folder; returns the catalog asset URNs stored under the project folder for QA and hand-off.
Pull inline previews of the six white-BG JPEGs to QA that every background is pure #FFFFFF, shadow-free, and the set reads as one consistent catalog before hand-off.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chaining throughout. Six parallel per-SKU sub-chains each run the same isolate-on-white pipeline, seeded only from that SKU's raw frame, and each produces BOTH deliverables from one graded master. Hero sku01 (n2-10): raw -> auto_straighten(2) -> auto_tone(3) -> color_temperature(4) -> brightness_contrast(5) => graded master, which forks into the white-BG path [select_subject(6) -> invert_selection(7) -> fill_area #FFFFFF on the background mask(8) -> crop_and_resize 3000(9)] and the transparent-PNG path [remove_background(10)]. sku02 (11-17), sku03 (18-23), sku04 (24-29), sku05 (30-36, with an extra dark_portions lift so the oxidised detail survives) and sku06 (37-42) mirror the same select->invert->fill->crop white-BG build plus a remove_background branch, with per-image white-balance direction. The white backdrop is never made by remove_background+fill; it is built the palette-correct way (subject selection inverted to a background mask, then solid #FFFFFF fill of that mask), matching the reference bg_removal_batch / product_ecom_whitebg specs. asset_create_folders(1) provides the destination folder consumed at finalize(44). All twelve finished deliverables (six transparent PNGs + six 3000 white-BG JPEGs) converge into upload_session(43) -> finalize(44) -> inline_preview QA(45), which verifies every background is pure #FFFFFF, shadow-free, and the set reads as one consistent catalog before hand-off. Nothing after the raw input loads and the folder-create originates outside the chain; the matched catalog set emerges only from the assembled pipeline.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon batch flagship: 45 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools, far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running six separate SKUs through an identical multi-step isolate-on-white pipeline while handling per-image variance (warm cast on some, cool on others; an oxidised piece needing a dark-portions lift so detail survives; a chain whose link-gaps must land in the background mask and not be painted as metal; a reflective solitaire whose specular edge must be retained), each SKU forking one graded master into a select->invert->solid-fill white-BG JPEG and a remove_background transparent PNG, then converging twelve deliverables into folder storage and an inline-preview QA pass. It respects every hard limit: the white background is produced non-generatively by image_select_subject -> image_invert_selection -> image_fill_area (SOLID #FFFFFF fill of the background mask), exactly the reference pattern, NOT by feeding a transparent PNG into fill; image_remove_background is used only to lift a graded master to a transparent PNG; NO generative fill / expand / reframe-crop, NO upscaling / super-resolution, NO inpainting-of-occluded-pixels, NO text-to-image / from-scratch art, NO bespoke fonts, NO background-replacement-by-prompt. The pieces are inspected via asset_inline_preview, not regenerated.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used: no image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo; no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting of occluded pixels; no bespoke font/letterform design; no background-replacement-by-prompt; no unreachable external account. All 45 steps use only the sanctioned palette.
reverify.notes: CORRECTED from the authored draft, which had two coherence/feasibility defects: (a) it fed image_remove_background's transparent-PNG output into image_fill_area to 'lay a backdrop behind' the piece, but image_fill_area fills a SELECTED region and there is no active background selection after remove_background; (b) it omitted image_invert_selection entirely. The white backdrop is now built the palette-correct, non-generative way used by the reference bg_removal_batch / product_ecom_whitebg specs (AO-00, AO-04, AO-39): image_select_subject -> image_invert_selection (background-only mask) -> image_fill_area (SOLID #FFFFFF fill of that mask) -> image_crop_and_resize to 3000x3000. image_fill_area (n8,15,21,27,34,40) is SOLID #FFFFFF fill of a real inverted background mask only - no generated content. image_remove_background (n10,17,23,29,36,42) lifts each SKU's SAME graded master to a TRANSPARENT PNG (its only sanctioned output), never reconstructing hidden pixels or compositing a scene; the two deliverables per SKU therefore carry an identical grade. All grade steps (auto_straighten, apply_auto_tone, adjust_color_temperature, adjust_brightness_and_contrast, adjust_dark_portions) are non-generative raster adjustments. asset_inline_preview only INSPECTS the uploaded results for QA. distinct_adobe_tools counts the 14 adobe-server tools (all 14 entries of tools_used). tool_call_count = 45 = number of connector_workflow steps. Category bg_removal_batch, operation O2, family Photo & Image, output_modality image, workflow_nature edit, vertical jewelry_luxury_goods all match the slot; brand 'Rukmini Silverworks' is a fictional, IP-clean persona replacing the real Nagpur client.
